summary: Dalam pemilihan pendahuluan New York, kandidat progresif yang didukung oleh Wali Kota Mamdani menyapu bersih tiga distrik, mengalahkan dua petahana, menyoroti perpecahan di Partai Demokrat mengenai isu Israel. Sementara itu, Senat mengesahkan resolusi kekuasaan perang, dan Trump akan bertemu dengan anggota Partai Republik di Kongres. Acara ini juga membahas perebutan sayap kiri dan kanan Partai Demokrat, ancaman keamanan siber, dan kemunduran warisan politik keluarga Kennedy.

vocabulary.cn: 权力掮客；能影响政治决策的人
vocabulary.en: a person who influences political decisions, often behind the scenes
vocabulary.excerpt: Today in the group chat is on Mamdani, the Democrats' new power broker.
vocabulary.example_cn: 今天群聊的话题是马姆达尼，民主党新的权力掮客。

vocabulary.en: supported, especially financially or politically
vocabulary.excerpt: Candidates he backed made a big statement in New York last night.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他支持的候选人在昨晚的纽约初选中发出了强烈的信号。

vocabulary.en: to give new and improved form, structure, or appearance to
vocabulary.excerpt: Revamp and recharge the Democratic Party in Washington that has, in so many ways, failed to meet the moment.
vocabulary.example_cn: 改造并重振华盛顿的民主党，该党在许多方面未能应对当下的挑战。

vocabulary.cn: 严峻的；明显的；赤裸裸的
vocabulary.en: severe or bare in appearance or outline; unpleasantly clear
vocabulary.excerpt: And a stark intelligence warning. The next major cyberattack could be just months away.
vocabulary.example_cn: 还有一个严峻的情报警告：下一次重大网络攻击可能就在几个月后。

vocabulary.en: an attempt to damage, disrupt, or gain unauthorized access to a computer system or network
vocabulary.excerpt: The next major cyberattack could be just months away.
vocabulary.example_cn: 下一次重大网络攻击可能就在几个月后。

vocabulary.cn: 卡米洛特；常指肯尼迪政府时期（1960-1963）的理想化时代
vocabulary.en: the legendary court of King Arthur; metaphorically, the Kennedy administration era
vocabulary.excerpt: And JFK's only grandson just lost his primary in New York. Where does that leave the legacy that a lot of people used to call Camelot?
vocabulary.example_cn: 肯尼迪唯一的孙子刚刚在纽约初选中落败。这给许多人曾称之为'卡米洛特'的遗产留下了什么？

vocabulary.en: a complete victory or success in all contests or areas
vocabulary.excerpt: A clean sweep for Zohran Mamdani.
vocabulary.example_cn: 佐赫兰·马姆达尼大获全胜。

vocabulary.cn: 进步派（的）；主张社会改革的人
vocabulary.en: a person advocating for social reform or change, often left-leaning
vocabulary.excerpt: The three progressives who were backed by New York City's Democratic socialist mayor, all of them won their House primaries last night.
vocabulary.example_cn: 三位由纽约市民主社会主义市长支持的进步派候选人，昨晚都赢得了众议院初选。

vocabulary.cn: to remove from office; to expel; to replace
vocabulary.en: removed from a position of power or authority
vocabulary.excerpt: former city comptroller Brad Lander ousted two-term congressman Dan Goldman after a bruising campaign
vocabulary.example_cn: Former city comptroller Brad Lander defeated two-term Rep. Dan Goldman after a fierce campaign.

vocabulary.en: physically or emotionally damaging; very competitive and tough
vocabulary.excerpt: after a bruising campaign that focused heavily on their differences over Israel
vocabulary.example_cn: After a fierce campaign largely centered on divisions over Israel

vocabulary.en: to accept and deal with something difficult or unpleasant
vocabulary.excerpt: We have to face up to it squarely.
vocabulary.example_cn: We have to face it.

vocabulary.en: a complete and disastrous failure
vocabulary.excerpt: Joe Biden's 'hug Bibi' strategy was a catastrophic failure.
vocabulary.example_cn: Joe Biden's 'Hug Bibi' strategy was a disastrous failure.

vocabulary.en: the person currently holding a political office
vocabulary.excerpt: defeating incumbent Adriano Espaillat
vocabulary.example_cn: Defeated incumbent Rep. Adriano Espaillat

vocabulary.en: the conservative or traditional members of a group or organization
vocabulary.excerpt: So, it was a rough night for the old guard in New York.
vocabulary.example_cn: Jadi, bagi kaum konservatif di New York, ini adalah malam yang sulit.

vocabulary.cn: pertanda; firasat (berasal dari ramalan daun teh)
vocabulary.en: signs or indications of future events, from tea-leaf reading
vocabulary.excerpt: everyone trying to turn these into tea leaves
vocabulary.example_cn: Setiap orang mencoba menafsirkan ini sebagai pertanda

vocabulary.en: to change the shape or structure of something
vocabulary.excerpt: A handful of primaries that go in one direction or the other in a given state or two aren't going to reshape who we are as House Democrats.
vocabulary.example_cn: Hasil dari beberapa pemilihan pendahuluan di satu atau dua negara bagian tidak akan membentuk ulang identitas kami sebagai Demokrat DPR.

vocabulary.en: strong and healthy; vigorous
vocabulary.excerpt: the robust, healthy turnout we saw expanding our traditional base in these primaries
vocabulary.example_cn: Tingkat partisipasi yang kuat dan sehat yang kita lihat dalam pemilihan pendahuluan ini sedang memperluas basis tradisional kita

vocabulary.en: the number of people who vote in an election or attend an event
vocabulary.excerpt: the robust, healthy turnout we saw expanding our traditional base in these primaries
vocabulary.example_cn: Tingkat partisipasi yang kuat dan sehat yang kita lihat dalam pemilihan pendahuluan ini sedang memperluas basis tradisional kita

vocabulary.en: a meal where guests serve themselves from a variety of dishes; metaphorically, a range of options
vocabulary.excerpt: the buffet known as the Democratic Party are adding some items
vocabulary.example_cn: 被称为民主党的自助餐正在增加一些菜品

vocabulary.cn: 挨家挨户敲门（竞选拉票）
vocabulary.en: to go from house to house, typically for political canvassing
vocabulary.excerpt: Political parties aren't perfect, but they're built by millions of people who knock doors, make calls, fight for the values they believe in.
vocabulary.example_cn: 政党并不完美，但它们是由数百万敲门、打电话、为他们信仰的价值观而奋斗的人建立起来的。

vocabulary.cn: 帮助某人成功；助其完成
vocabulary.en: to help someone succeed or complete a task, especially a political campaign
vocabulary.excerpt: If you don't believe in the party, then don't ask its members to carry you across the finish line.
vocabulary.example_cn: 如果你不相信这个政党，那就不要要求它的成员帮你冲过终点线。

vocabulary.cn: 成长中的烦恼；发展初期的问题
vocabulary.en: difficulties experienced in the early stages of development
vocabulary.excerpt: We are experiencing growing pains.
vocabulary.example_cn: 我们正在经历成长的烦恼。

vocabulary.cn: 摇摆选区；两党竞争激烈的选区
vocabulary.en: a district where voters are not strongly aligned with one party, often deciding elections
vocabulary.excerpt: a swing district in New York that will help us get the majority
vocabulary.example_cn: 纽约的一个摇摆选区，将帮助我们获得多数席位

vocabulary.en: a test that uses a single indicator to determine a person's stance on a broader issue
vocabulary.excerpt: Israel is becoming a litmus test for many, especially in these deep-blue districts.
vocabulary.example_cn: 以色列问题正成为许多人的试金石，尤其是在这些深蓝选区。

sentences.en: Candidates he backed made a big statement in New York last night.
sentences.cn: 他支持的候选人在昨晚的纽约取得了重大胜利。
sentences.structure: 主句：Candidates made a big statement；定语从句：he backed（省略that）。
sentences.analysis: make a big statement 意为"发出强烈信号，取得重大成果"，是新闻常用表达。定语从句省略关系代词that，需识别先行词Candidates。

sentences.en: The three progressives who were backed by New York City's Democratic socialist mayor, all of them won their House primaries last night.
sentences.cn: 三位由纽约市民主社会主义市长支持的进步派候选人，昨晚都赢得了众议院初选。
sentences.structure: 主句：The three progressives won their House primaries；定语从句：who were backed by...；同位语：all of them。
sentences.analysis: 同位语结构all of them重复主语，起强调作用。House primaries指"众议院初选"。

sentences.en: So, you had former city comptroller Brad Lander ousted two-term congressman Dan Goldman after a bruising campaign that focused heavily on their differences over Israel.
sentences.cn: 因此，前市审计长布拉德·兰德在一场激烈且主要围绕以色列问题分歧的竞选后，击败了两任国会议员丹·戈德曼。
sentences.structure: 主句：you had Brad Lander ousted Dan Goldman；定语从句：that focused heavily on...；时间状语：after a bruising campaign。
sentences.analysis: have sb. ousted sb. 结构意为"使某人击败某人"。bruising campaign指"激烈、伤感情的竞选"。focus heavily on强调"主要聚焦于"。

sentences.en: Our party needs to admit that Joe Biden's 'hug Bibi' strategy was a catastrophic failure.
sentences.cn: 我们的党需要承认，乔·拜登的"拥抱比比"策略是一场灾难性的失败。
sentences.structure: 主句：Our party needs to admit；宾语从句：that Joe Biden's strategy was a catastrophic failure。
sentences.analysis: hug Bibi是新闻压缩表达，Bibi指以色列总理内塔尼亚胡，hug Bibi意为"拥抱内塔尼亚胡"，指拜登对以色列的亲密政策。catastrophic failure"灾难性失败"。

sentences.en: And state Assemblywoman Claire Valdez, a Mamdani-backed Democratic socialist, will be the Democratic nominee for New York's 7th congressional district.
sentences.cn: 州议员克莱尔·瓦尔迪兹，一位由曼达尼支持的民主社会主义者，将成为纽约第七国会选区的民主党提名人。
sentences.structure: 主句：Claire Valdez will be the Democratic nominee；同位语：a Mamdani-backed Democratic socialist。
sentences.analysis: 同位语提供额外身份信息。Mamdani-backed是复合形容词，意为"曼达尼支持的"。congressional district"国会选区"。

sentences.en: And Darializa Avila Chevalier, another Democratic socialist, captured New York's 13th District, defeating incumbent Adriano Espaillat.
sentences.cn: captured意为"赢得（席位）"。incumbent指"现任议员"。defeating是现在分词作伴随状语，表示结果。
sentences.structure: 主句：Darializa Avila Chevalier captured New York's 13th District；同位语：another Democratic socialist；伴随状语：defeating incumbent...。

sentences.en: Politics like sports, has seasons of disappointment and seasons of triumph.
sentences.cn: 政治就像体育，有失望的季节，也有胜利的季节。
sentences.structure: 主句：Politics has seasons；插入语：like sports。
sentences.analysis: like sports是插入语，将政治比作体育。seasons of disappointment/triumph是比喻，指"低谷期和高峰期"。

sentences.en: What matters is that we continue showing up.
sentences.cn: 重要的是我们继续出现。
sentences.structure: 主语从句：What matters；系表结构：is that...；表语从句：that we continue showing up。
sentences.analysis: What matters是主语从句，意为"重要的是"。show up意为"露面、出现"，在此语境中指"继续参与政治"。

sentences.en: You had someone like hakeem Jeffries on Wednesday, sort of implying like, hey, settle down, everyone trying to turn these into tea leaves.
sentences.cn: 周三，像哈基姆·杰弗里斯这样的人，似乎在暗示：嘿，冷静点，每个人都试图把这些解读为预兆。
sentences.structure: 主句：You had someone；同位语：like hakeem Jeffries；现在分词短语：sort of implying...；直接引语：hey, settle down...；伴随状语：everyone trying to turn these into tea leaves。
sentences.analysis: sort of implying意为"有点暗示"。turn these into tea leaves是习语，tea leaves指"茶叶占卜"，引申为"解读预兆"。

sentences.en: A handful of primaries that go in one direction or the other in a given state or two aren't going to reshape who we are as House Democrats.
sentences.cn: 一两个州中少数几场初选的结果，无论方向如何，都不会重塑我们作为众议院民主党人的身份。
sentences.structure: 主句：A handful of primaries aren't going to reshape；定语从句：that go in one direction or the other；宾语从句：who we are as House Democrats。
sentences.analysis: a handful of意为"少数"。go in one direction or the other指"朝某个方向发展"。reshape who we as...意为"重塑我们作为...的身份"。

sentences.en: I think that the big takeaway from last night was the robust, healthy turnout we saw expanding our traditional base in these primaries, which means that we have room to grow.
sentences.cn: 我认为昨晚的重要收获是，我们看到了强劲、健康的投票率，这扩大了我们在这些初选中的传统基础，这意味着我们有增长空间。
sentences.structure: 主句：I think；宾语从句：that the big takeaway was the turnout；定语从句：we saw；现在分词短语：expanding our traditional base；非限制性定语从句：which means that...。
sentences.analysis: takeaway意为"要点、收获"。robust, healthy turnout指"强劲健康的投票率"。expanding是现在分词作结果状语。have room to grow意为"有增长空间"。

sentences.en: I think that means that the buffet known as the Democratic Party are adding some items.
sentences.cn: 我认为这意味着被称为民主党的自助餐正在增加一些菜品。
sentences.structure: 主句：I think；宾语从句：that means；表语从句：that the buffet are adding some items；过去分词短语：known as the Democratic Party。
sentences.analysis: buffet是比喻，将民主党比作自助餐，adding some items指"增加新元素或候选人"。known as意为"被称为"。

sentences.en: Political parties aren't perfect, but they're built by millions of people who knock doors, make calls, fight for the values they believe in.
sentences.cn: 政党并不完美，但它们是由数百万敲门、打电话、为他们信仰的价值观而奋斗的人建立起来的。
sentences.structure: 并列句：Political parties aren't perfect, but they're built；定语从句：who knock doors, make calls, fight for the values；定语从句：they believe in（省略that）。
sentences.analysis: knock doors指"上门拉票"，make calls指"打电话拉票"，是竞选活动常见表达。fight for the values they believe in中，they believe in是省略that的定语从句修饰values。

sentences.en: If you don't believe in the party, then don't ask its members to carry you across the finish line.
sentences.cn: 如果你不相信这个党，那就不要要求它的成员带你冲过终点线。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句：If you don't believe in the party；主句：don't ask its members to carry you across the finish line。
sentences.analysis: carry you across the finish line是比喻，意为"帮你赢得选举"。finish line指"终点线"，在此指"选举胜利"。

sentences.en: Who is the Democratic Party, if not the voters?
sentences.cn: 如果不是选民，民主党又是什么呢？
sentences.structure: 主句：Who is the Democratic Party；条件状语从句：if not the voters。
sentences.analysis: if not the voters是省略结构，完整为if it is not the voters，意为"如果不是选民的话"。反问句强调选民才是政党的核心。

sentences.en: And party leaders need to listen to what voters are telling us.
sentences.cn: 而政党领导人需要倾听选民在告诉我们什么。
sentences.structure: 主句：party leaders need to listen to；宾语从句：what voters are telling us。
sentences.analysis: listen to what...是"倾听...的内容"。what引导宾语从句，在从句中作telling的直接宾语。

sentences.en: And right now, they're demanding our party be bolder.
sentences.cn: 而现在，他们要求我们的党更大胆。
sentences.structure: 主句：they're demanding；宾语从句：our party be bolder（省略should）。
sentences.analysis: demand后接宾语从句时，从句谓语用虚拟语气（should+动词原形，should可省略）。be bolder意为"更大胆"。

sentences.en: We are experiencing growing pains.
sentences.cn: 我们正在经历成长的阵痛。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾结构。
sentences.analysis: growing pains是固定短语，原指"生长痛"，比喻"发展过程中遇到的困难"。

sentences.en: But what I'll tell you, what I will tell you is that the big takeaway from the New York primaries last night was New York 17, a moderate candidate who will take on Mike Lawler, a swing district in New York that will help us get the majority.
sentences.cn: 但我要告诉你的是，昨晚纽约初选的重要收获是纽约第17选区，一位温和派候选人将挑战迈克·劳勒，这是一个摇摆选区，将帮助我们获得多数席位。
sentences.structure: 主句：what I will tell you is that...；表语从句：that the big takeaway was New York 17；同位语：a moderate candidate；定语从句：who will take on Mike Lawler；同位语：a swing district；定语从句：that will help us get the majority。
sentences.analysis: what I will tell you是主语从句。take on意为"挑战（对手）"。swing district指"摇摆选区"，即两党竞争激烈的选区。get the majority指"获得多数席位"。

sentences.en: Democrats -- progressive winning in Democratic safe seats is not some narrative that we should just be flashing the lights about.
sentences.cn: 民主党 --  -- 进步派在民主党安全席位中获胜，并不是我们应该大肆宣扬的叙事。
sentences.structure: 主句：progressive winning is not some narrative；定语从句：that we should just be flashing the lights about。
sentences.analysis: safe seats指"安全席位"，即某党稳赢的选区。flashing the lights about是比喻，意为"大肆宣传、引起注意"。narrative指"叙事、说法"。

topics.title: 纽约初选与民主党内部左转
topics.content: 2026年6月23日纽约初选中，纽约市长佐赫兰·马姆达尼（Zohran Mamdani）支持的进步派候选人赢得三个众议院选区，击败两名现任议员。这反映了民主党内部在以色列政策等问题上的分歧，以及进步派势力的上升。马姆达尼是民主社会主义者，其支持被视为对党内建制派的挑战。
topics.keywords: Zohran Mamdani · 进步派 · 初选 · 民主社会主义

topics.title: 以色列问题成为民主党试金石
topics.content: 在纽约初选中，候选人布拉德·兰德（Brad Lander）批评拜登的'拥抱比比'（hug Bibi）战略是灾难性失败，凸显以色列-巴勒斯坦问题成为民主党内部关键分歧。进步派主张对以色列采取更强硬立场，而建制派则支持传统盟友关系。这一议题可能影响2026年中期选举。
topics.keywords: Israel · litmus test · hug Bibi · 拜登

topics.title: 网络安全威胁与人工智能
topics.content: 节目中提及一份严峻的情报警告：下一次重大网络攻击可能就在几个月后，且人类可能与之无关。这暗示人工智能驱动的网络攻击日益成为现实威胁。近年来，AI被用于自动化网络钓鱼、恶意软件生成等，加剧了安全挑战。
topics.keywords: cyberattack · 人工智能 · 情报警告 · 网络安全

topics.title: 肯尼迪家族政治遗产的衰落
topics.content: 约翰·F·肯尼迪唯一的孙子在纽约初选中落败，引发对'卡米洛特'（Camelot）时代遗产的讨论。肯尼迪家族曾是美国政治象征，但近年来其成员在选举中屡遭挫折，反映了政治王朝影响力的减弱以及选民对传统政治家族的疏离。
topics.keywords: Camelot · 肯尼迪家族 · 政治遗产 · 初选

quiz.question: 在原文 'Candidates he backed made a big statement in New York last night.' 中，'backed' 最接近的含义是？
quiz.explanation: 在政治语境中，'backed' 意为'支持'，指候选人得到某人的背书或资助。其他选项不符合上下文。

quiz.question: 短语 'clean sweep' 在 'A clean sweep for Zohran Mamdani.' 中的意思是？
quiz.explanation: 'clean sweep' 是固定搭配，意为'全面胜利'，指在所有竞争中都获胜。这里指马姆达尼支持的候选人全部赢得初选。

quiz.question: 原文 'Israel is becoming a litmus test for many' 中，'litmus test' 指的是？
quiz.explanation: 'litmus test' 是比喻用法，意为'试金石'，指用单一问题来检验一个人的政治立场。这里指以色列问题成为判断民主党候选人立场的标准。

quiz.question: 根据原文，纽约初选结果主要反映了什么？
options: A. Partai Demokrat secara keseluruhan bergeser ke kanan
B. Pengaruh progresif meningkat di daerah pemilihan yang aman
C. Partai Republik mencapai terobosan di New York
D. Dukungan pemilih terhadap Trump meningkat
quiz.explanation: 原文指出马姆达尼支持的进步派候选人在深蓝选区获胜，击败了现任议员，表明进步派在安全选区影响力上升。但分析也强调，决定多数席位的仍是温和派候选人。

quiz.question: Manakah pernyataan berikut yang benar tentang perdebatan internal Partai Demokrat?
options: A. Semua anggota Partai Demokrat mendukung kebijakan Israel Biden
B. Masalah Israel telah menjadi titik fokus perpecahan di dalam partai
C. Kaum progresif dan establishment sepenuhnya setuju dalam masalah Israel
D. Pemilih tidak peduli dengan kebijakan luar negeri
quiz.explanation: Dalam teks asli, Rand mengkritik strategi 'peluk Bibi' Biden, dan acara tersebut membahas Israel sebagai 'litmus test', yang menunjukkan bahwa masalah Israel adalah perpecahan kunci di dalam partai. Opsi lain bertentangan dengan teks asli.

quiz.question: Acara tersebut menyebutkan warisan 'Camelot', apa latar belakangnya?
options: C. Nama tempat di New York
quiz.explanation: 'Camelot' dalam konteks politik secara khusus merujuk pada masa kepresidenan John F. Kennedy (1961-1963), melambangkan idealisme dan pesona politik. Teks asli menyebutkan cucu Kennedy kalah dalam pemilihan, memicu diskusi tentang warisan 'Camelot'.

paragraphs.speaker: Cnn Anchor
paragraphs.text: But according to Ross Township Police Department, that fawn went over the side of a retaining wall at a high school on Monday. Officer Roy and his sergeant came to the rescue. Good job there. They were able to take care of the matter and safely, as well. That does it for CNN HEADLINE EXPRESS. I'm Brad Smith. CNN THIS MORNING with Audie Cornish starts right now.
paragraphs.cn: Namun menurut Departemen Kepolisian Kota Ross, pada hari Senin, anak rusa itu jatuh dari dinding penahan di sebuah sekolah menengah. Petugas Roy dan kepala polisinya datang untuk menyelamatkan. Kerja bagus. Mereka berhasil menangani situasi dengan aman. Ini adalah akhir dari CNN Headline News. Saya Brad Smith. Acara CNN This Morning yang dipandu oleh Audie Cornish dimulai sekarang.

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: Today in the group chat is on Mamdani, the Democrats' new power broker. Candidates he backed made a big statement in New York last night. BRAD LANDER (D),
paragraphs.cn: Topik obrolan hari ini adalah Mamdani, raja kekuasaan baru Partai Demokrat. Kandidat yang didukungnya membuat pernyataan penting di New York tadi malam. Brad Lander (Demokrat):

paragraphs.speaker: New York House Candidate
paragraphs.text: Revamp and recharge the Democratic Party in Washington that has, in so many ways, failed to meet the moment.
paragraphs.cn: Merombak dan merevitalisasi Partai Demokrat di Washington, yang dalam banyak hal gagal menghadapi tantangan saat ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And a stark intelligence warning. The next major cyberattack could be just months away. And humans may have little to do with it. And JFK's only grandson just lost his primary in New York. Where does that leave the legacy that a lot of people used to call Camelot? And some things Zohran Mamdani and Donald Trump can agree on: a big triumph in Washington for an issue that couldn't be closer to home. MAYOR ZOHRAN MAMDANI (D),
paragraphs.cn: Dan peringatan intelijen yang serius: serangan siber besar berikutnya mungkin hanya beberapa bulan lagi, dan manusia mungkin tidak ada hubungannya dengan itu. Juga, satu-satunya cucu Kennedy baru saja kalah dalam pemilihan pendahuluan New York. Apa artinya ini bagi warisan yang oleh banyak orang disebut 'Camelot'? Dan beberapa hal yang disepakati oleh Zohran Mamdani dan Donald Trump: kemenangan besar di Washington untuk isu yang dekat dengan keluarga. Wali Kota Zohran Mamdani (Demokrat):

paragraphs.speaker: New York City
paragraphs.text: The old world is dying, and the new world struggles to be born.
paragraphs.cn: Dunia lama sedang sekarat, dunia baru berjuang untuk lahir.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: A clean sweep for Zohran Mamdani. Good morning, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish, and here's what we're talking about today. The three progressives who were backed by New York City's Democratic socialist mayor, all of them won their House primaries last night. So, you had former city comptroller Brad Lander ousted two-term congressman Dan Goldman after a bruising campaign that focused heavily on their differences over Israel.
paragraphs.cn: Zohran Mamdani meraih kemenangan besar. Selamat pagi semuanya. Saya Audie Cornish, dan inilah yang akan kita bahas hari ini. Tiga kandidat progresif yang didukung oleh wali kota sosialis Demokrat New York, semuanya menang dalam pemilihan pendahuluan DPR tadi malam. Mantan auditor kota Brad Lander mengalahkan anggota DPR dua periode Dan Goldman setelah kampanye sengit yang berfokus pada perbedaan mereka mengenai Israel.

paragraphs.speaker: Lander
paragraphs.text: Democrats are painfully divided by our differences over the U.S. relationship to Israel and Palestine, and we have to face up to it squarely.
paragraphs.cn: Partai Demokrat terpecah belah karena perbedaan pendapat kita tentang hubungan AS dengan Israel dan Palestina, dan kita harus menghadapinya.

paragraphs.speaker: Lander
paragraphs.text: Our party needs to admit that Joe Biden's "hug Bibi" strategy was a catastrophic failure.
paragraphs.cn: Partai kita perlu mengakui bahwa strategi 'peluk Bibi' Joe Biden adalah kegagalan yang membawa bencana.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And state Assemblywoman Claire Valdez, a Mamdani-backed Democratic socialist, will be the Democratic nominee for New York's 7th congressional district. And Darializa Avila Chevalier, another Democratic socialist, captured New York's 13th District, defeating incumbent Adriano Espaillat. So, it was a rough night for the old guard in New York. There are two incumbents who were defeated. REP. DAN GOLDMAN (D),
paragraphs.cn: Anggota DPR negara bagian Claire Valdez, seorang sosialis demokrat yang didukung Mamdani, akan menjadi kandidat Partai Demokrat untuk Distrik Kongres ke-7 New York. Sosialis demokrat lainnya, Daria Liza Avila Chevalier, memenangkan Distrik ke-13 New York, mengalahkan petahana Adriano Espaillat. Jadi, ini malam yang sulit bagi kaum konservatif di New York. Dua petahana dikalahkan. Anggota DPR Dan Goldman (Demokrat):

paragraphs.speaker: New York
paragraphs.text: Politics like sports, has seasons of disappointment and seasons of triumph. What matters is that we continue showing up. We continue believing. We continue fighting for something bigger than just ourselves. And so, while my service in Congress may end at the end of this year, my commitment to these ideals will not.
paragraphs.cn: Politik itu seperti olahraga, ada musim kekecewaan dan musim kemenangan. Yang penting adalah kita terus hadir, terus percaya, terus berjuang untuk sesuatu yang lebih besar dari diri kita sendiri. Jadi, meskipun masa jabatan saya di Kongres mungkin berakhir tahun ini, komitmen saya terhadap cita-cita ini tidak akan berubah.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Joining me now in the group chat, Ron Brownstein, CNN senior political analyst; Jarrett Stepman, contributor at "The Daily Signal"; and Antjuan Seawright, Democratic Party strategist. Antjuan, I'm going to start with you, because this is all about Democrats last night and the Democratic Party, except it kind of was about the Democratic socialists instead. You had someone like hakeem Jeffries on Wednesday, sort of implying like, hey, settle down, everyone trying to turn these into tea leaves. Here he is. REP.
paragraphs.cn: Bergabung dalam obrolan kami sekarang: Analis Politik Senior CNN Ron Brownstein, kontributor The Daily Signal Jarrett Stepman, dan ahli strategi Demokrat Antwan Seawright. Antwan, saya mulai dengan Anda, karena semalam semuanya tentang Partai Demokrat, tapi sepertinya berubah menjadi tentang sosialis demokrat. Pada hari Rabu, orang seperti Hakeem Jeffries sepertinya mengisyaratkan: hei, tenang, jangan baca semua ini sebagai pertanda. Inilah yang dia katakan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: HAKEEM JEFFRIES (D-NY): There are 215 members in the House Democratic Caucus. A handful of primaries that go in one direction or the other in a given state or two aren't going to reshape who we are as House Democrats.
paragraphs.cn: Hakeem Jeffries (D-NY): Fraksi DPR Partai Demokrat memiliki 215 anggota. Beberapa pemilihan pendahuluan di satu atau dua negara bagian yang bergerak ke arah tertentu tidak akan membentuk kembali identitas kami sebagai anggota DPR dari Partai Demokrat.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Is this denying the reality of where voters are headed?
paragraphs.cn: Apakah ini mengingkari realitas pergerakan pemilih?

paragraphs.speaker: Antjuan Seawright
paragraphs.text: A few takeaways from last night. One, I think that these New York primaries are not some big, bold -- big, bold print or summary of where the totality of the Democratic Party is heading. No. 2, I would argue that the big takeaway from last night was the robust, healthy turnout we saw expanding our traditional base in these primaries, which means that we have room to grow. I think that means that the buffet known as the Democratic Party are adding some items.
paragraphs.cn: Ada beberapa hal yang bisa dipetik dari semalam. Pertama, saya pikir pemilihan pendahuluan New York ini bukanlah tanda atau ringkasan yang besar dan jelas tentang arah Partai Demokrat secara keseluruhan. Kedua, saya pikir hal terbesar yang bisa dipetik dari semalam adalah kita melihat partisipasi pemilih yang kuat dan sehat dalam pemilihan pendahuluan ini, yang memperluas basis tradisional kita, artinya kita memiliki ruang untuk tumbuh. Saya pikir ini berarti prasmanan yang disebut Partai Demokrat sedang menambah beberapa hidangan.

paragraphs.speaker: Antjuan Seawright
paragraphs.text: And so that means more people will stand in line to eat from the buffet in November.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi ini berarti lebih banyak orang akan mengantre untuk prasmanan pada bulan November.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. It sounds like a parallel buffet. No, no, no. Before you go, Jamie Harrison, Democrat, he was saying -- I think this is 23rd, almost midnight. He's tweeting this: "Political parties aren't perfect, but they're built by millions of people who knock doors, make calls, fight for the values they believe in." And then he says this, because a lot of people are -- are criticizing the party.
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Kedengarannya seperti prasmanan paralel. Tidak, tidak, tidak. Sebelum Anda pergi, Demokrat Jamie Harrison berkata -- -- Saya pikir ini tanggal 23, menjelang tengah malam. Dia tweet: 'Partai tidak sempurna, tetapi mereka dibangun oleh jutaan orang yang mengetuk pintu, menelepon, dan berjuang untuk nilai-nilai yang mereka yakini.' Lalu dia berkata seperti ini, karena banyak orang mengkritik partai ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: "If you don't believe in the party, then don't ask its members to carry you across the finish line." Chris Murphy responds, Chris Murphy of Connecticut. And he goes, "I don't know, man. Who is the Democratic Party, if not the voters? Democratic voters choose candidates, not party leaders. And party leaders need to listen to what voters are telling us. And right now, they're demanding our party be bolder."
paragraphs.cn: 'Jika Anda tidak percaya pada partai ini, jangan minta anggotanya membawa Anda melewati garis finis.' Chris Murphy dari Connecticut menanggapi. Dia berkata: 'Entahlah, kawan. Jika pemilih bukan Partai Demokrat, lalu siapa? Pemilih Partai Demokrat yang memilih kandidat, bukan pemimpin partai. Pemimpin partai perlu mendengarkan apa yang pemilih katakan kepada kita. Sekarang, mereka menuntut partai kita lebih berani.'

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: Sure. We are experiencing growing pains. That happens on both sides of the aisle. But what I'll tell you, what I will tell you is that the big takeaway from the New York primaries last night was New York 17, a moderate candidate who will take on Mike Lawler, a swing district in New York that will help us get the majority. Democrats -- progressive winning in Democratic safe seats is not some narrative that we should just be flashing the lights about. I mean, that's healthy debate. That's healthy within the party.
paragraphs.cn: Tentu. Kita sedang mengalami sakit tumbuh. Ini terjadi di kedua partai. Tapi saya akan memberitahu Anda, hal terbesar dari pemilihan pendahuluan New York semalam adalah Distrik ke-17 New York, di mana seorang kandidat moderat akan menantang Mike Lawler, sebuah distrik ayunan yang akan membantu kita meraih mayoritas. Partai Demokrat -- -- kemenangan progresif di kursi aman Demokrat bukanlah hal yang perlu dibesar-besarkan. Maksud saya, ini adalah debat yang sehat. Ini adalah fenomena sehat di dalam partai.

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: I'm more focused on how do we get to 218? And New York 17 primary is a direct result of that.
paragraphs.cn: Saya lebih fokus pada bagaimana kita mencapai 218 kursi? Dan pemilihan pendahuluan Distrik ke-17 New York adalah hasil langsung dari tujuan itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Ron Brownstein
paragraphs.text: It is really a two-track story. I think that it is not as simple as being portrayed. Antjuan was right. I mean, in safe Democratic seats, in blue places, there is no question the party is moving to the left. If you are an incumbent, kind of a mainstream Democrat in a solidly blue district, especially in an urban setting, you have to be looking over your left shoulder after what we are watching this year.
paragraphs.cn: Ini sebenarnya cerita dua jalur. Saya pikir tidak sesederhana yang digambarkan. Antwan benar. Maksud saya, di kursi aman Demokrat, daerah biru, tidak diragukan lagi partai bergerak ke kiri. Jika Anda seorang petahana Demokrat arus utama di daerah biru yang solid, terutama di lingkungan perkotaan, setelah melihat apa yang terjadi tahun ini, Anda harus waspada terhadap tantangan dari kiri.

paragraphs.speaker: Ron Brownstein
paragraphs.text: But if you look at the districts that are going to decide which party has the majority with a few exceptions, Democrats are still relying primarily on moderate nominees. I mean, New York 17, South Carolina 1 and Utah 1 last night. We didn't say Ben McAdams. We didn't say Nancy Lacore. We didn't say Kate Connelly. But ultimately, those are the candidates that are going to decide whether -- which party is in the majority.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi jika Anda melihat distrik-distrik yang akan menentukan partai mana yang menjadi mayoritas, dengan sedikit pengecualian, Demokrat masih sangat bergantung pada kandidat moderat. Maksud saya, semalam di Distrik ke-17 New York, Distrik ke-1 South Carolina, dan Distrik ke-1 Utah. Kami tidak menyebut Ben McAdams. Kami tidak menyebut Nancy LaCorte. Kami tidak menyebut Kate Connelly. Tapi pada akhirnya, kandidat-kandidat inilah yang akan menentukan partai mana yang menjadi mayoritas.

paragraphs.speaker: Ron Brownstein
paragraphs.text: Now, it is not without consequence that these left candidates are winning in, even in safe places, because A, Republicans are going to try to tie all Democrats to them. And if -- if Democrats do get the majority, it's going to affect the internal dynamics of the caucus.
paragraphs.cn: Sekarang, kemenangan kandidat sayap kiri ini bahkan di kursi aman bukannya tanpa konsekuensi, karena A, Partai Republik akan mencoba mengaitkan semua Demokrat dengan mereka. Jika Demokrat memang meraih mayoritas, ini akan mempengaruhi dinamika di dalam fraksi.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Let me make sure -- let me make sure --
paragraphs.cn: Biarkan saya konfirmasi -- -- biarkan saya konfirmasi -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Brownstein
paragraphs.text: But in terms of who gets the majority, it's not critical.
paragraphs.cn: But in terms of who gets the majority, that's not crucial.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- Jarrett has a moment to step in here. Because this is the argument that I've heard over and over and over again with each one of these special elections. There's a moderate doing well. There's someone over here making noise. Why? Who are they? But you have Republicans using those voices to be like, look, they're all communists. And does that work against some of these moderate candidates? JARRETT STEPMAN, CONTRIBUTOR, "THE DAILY SIGNAL": Sure.
paragraphs.cn: -- Let Jarrett interject. Because this is an argument I hear repeatedly in every special election. There's a moderate who performed well. There's another person making noise here. Why? Who are they? But Republicans use these voices to say, look, they're all communists. Could this adversely affect certain moderate candidates? Daily Signal contributor Jarrett Stepman: Of course.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But you can't deny the fact that Mamdani and folks like him are trying to reshape the Democrat Party. I thought a lot of the issues were actually less just directed toward Donald Trump. What you see a lot of in the Democrat Party is opposition to Trump. This is about internal debates within the party. I mean, it's very clear now that Israel is becoming a litmus test for many, especially in these deep-blue districts. That clearly came to pass in these elections.
paragraphs.cn: But you can't deny that Mamdani and people like him are trying to reshape the Democratic Party. I think many issues are actually not just about Donald Trump. What you see a lot in the Democratic Party is opposition to Trump. This is about an internal debate within the party. I mean, it's clear now that Israel is becoming a litmus test for many, especially in these deep-blue districts. This was clearly reflected in this election.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, yes, I understand that the larger political picture, the national picture, is being shaped by a few more moderate swing districts. But this does shape the party, long-term. If you're sending more socialist members to Congress --
paragraphs.cn: So, yes, I understand the bigger political picture, the national picture, is shaped by some more moderate swing districts. But this does shape the party in the long run. If you send more socialists to Congress --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Are you saying that --
paragraphs.cn: Are you saying --

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.text: -- that changes the dynamic.
paragraphs.cn: -- that would change the dynamics.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- because of what happened with both the Tea Party and MAGA candidates.
paragraphs.cn: -- Because of the situation with the Tea Party and MAGA candidates.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- who also, at the time, were considered extreme, can't win generals. Why are they doing this? Where is the party going? Bush Republicans melting down, you know, like witches in "The Wizard of Oz." It was a whole thing.
paragraphs.cn: -- These people were also seen as extremists at the time, unlikely to win general support. Why would they do this? Where is this party going? The Bush-era Republicans are collapsing like the witch in The Wizard of Oz. It's a real mess.

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.text: It was transitionary.
paragraphs.cn: This is a transition period.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Are you speaking from experience?
paragraphs.cn: Are you speaking from experience?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Or are you poking at the weak spots?
paragraphs.cn: Or are you poking at their weaknesses?

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.text: No, it is experience. The populist politics of the right have reshaped the Republican Party. I think the Democrat Party is going through a similar period: that the establishment is seen as not being as credible. They've taken some lumps. They've gone against Trump and lost in 2024. Many of the Bernie Sanders-style politicians feel that they're -- they're in a good spot right now.
paragraphs.cn: No, it's experience. Right-wing populist politics has reshaped the Republican Party. I think the Democratic Party is going through a similar period: the establishment is seen as less credible. They've taken some hits. They fought against Trump and lost in 2024. Many Bernie Sanders-style politicians feel they are now in a favorable position.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: You just mentioned Bernie Sanders.
paragraphs.cn: You just mentioned Bernie Sanders.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Did he have a good night endorsing?
paragraphs.cn: Did his endorsement last night perform well?

paragraphs.speaker: Brownstein
paragraphs.text: Yes, sure. In New York, right? I mean, and in New York City. I mean, you know, and there are -- look, as I was saying, this is primarily happening in safe blue places so far. There are a few of the Sanders-Warren-type candidates who are -- who have gotten nominations in competitive races. Maine, Graham Platner, obviously, in the Senate race. There's a California -- the race for David Valadao's seat. There's going to be one in Colorado coming up.
paragraphs.cn: Yes, of course. In New York, right? I mean, in New York City. Look, as I said, so far this has mainly happened in safe blue districts. There are some Sanders-Warren-style candidates who have already won nominations in competitive races. For example, Graham Pratner in Maine, obviously in the Senate race. And California -- the race for David Valadao's seat. Colorado will also have one.

paragraphs.speaker: Brownstein
paragraphs.text: How they do in November is going to be really critical to the long- term --
paragraphs.cn: Their performance in November will be crucial for the long-term --

paragraphs.speaker: Brownstein
paragraphs.text: -- prospects of this movement. I mean, these -- the -- you know, all the districts we're talking about in New York City, Harris won at least 72 percent of the vote. OK, so these are -- these are very safe Democratic places. And if they do get to Washington, they are going to -- they are going to affect the internal dynamics of the caucus.
paragraphs.cn: -- prospects of this movement. I mean, these -- you know, all the districts we're talking about in New York City, Harris won at least 72% of the vote. So these are very safe Democratic territory. If they actually get to Washington, they will influence the dynamics within the caucus.

paragraphs.speaker: Brownstein
paragraphs.text: But ultimately, I think whether the left comes out of November with as much momentum as they look like this morning, I think it's going to depend a lot on how those candidates in swing districts do.
paragraphs.cn: But ultimately, I think whether the left will still have as much momentum after November as it seems this morning will largely depend on how candidates in those swing districts perform.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, momentum is fair, which is back to Antjuan's point.
paragraphs.cn: So, the momentum is there, which brings us back to Antjuan's point.

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: The through line has been economics, has been affordability. And I think, whether you talk about the progressive left or the centrist left or the moderates, however you want to describe them, the through line has been affordability. And I would just say that how you campaign and how you govern are two different things.
paragraphs.cn: The consistent thread throughout has been the economy, affordability. I think whether you're talking about the progressive left, the center-left, or the moderates, however you describe them, the thread is affordability. I just want to say, campaigning and governing are two different things.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. Mamdani's a good example.
paragraphs.cn: Yes. Mamdani is a good example.

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: And I think we're seeing that with Mayor Mamdani. He talked about a number of things on the campaign trail. But people in New York, by and large, have been very pleased with him. That's why the right have not been able to attack him and tried to mold him in to be something he's not.
paragraphs.cn: I think we saw that from Mayor Mamdani. He talked about many things during the campaign. But overall, the people of New York are very satisfied with him. That's why the right can't attack him or try to portray him as someone he's not.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK. We're going to talk more about some of these races, but I want to turn to one other thing, because I always like to look at where the money went. So, did big tech try to buy New York's political primary? How tech companies spent millions to shape Tuesday's vote. Plus, are immigration courts becoming ICE hunting grounds? A federal judge has decided to step in.
paragraphs.cn: Okay. We'll discuss some of these races further, but I want to turn to another thing because I always like to see where the money flows. So, are big tech companies trying to buy New York's political primaries? How did tech companies spend millions to influence Tuesday's vote? Also, are immigration courts becoming hunting grounds for ICE? A federal judge has decided to intervene.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: All right. It's now almost 15 minutes past the hour. This is your morning roundup. Bill Gates told congressional investigators he suspected he was in the presence of Jeffrey Epstein's victims. Gates testified before the House Oversight Committee in a closed-door hearing earlier this month. According to newly-released transcripts, Gates said he never interacted with the victims and never witnessed any sexual misconduct.
paragraphs.cn: Alright. It's now 15 minutes past the hour. This is your morning news roundup. Bill Gates told congressional investigators that he suspects he was once in the same room as one of Jeffrey Epstein's victims. Gates testified in a closed-door hearing before the House Oversight Committee earlier this month. According to newly released records, Gates said he never interacted with the victim and never witnessed any sexual misconduct.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: A federal judge has issued a nationwide block against the Trump administration's policy of making arrests at immigration courts. Federal officers began detaining court attendees last year under the Trump administration's policies. Now, this new ruling calls the practice arbitrary. And a wildfire in Utah has now burned about 31,000 acres. It's still not contained. High temperatures and strong winds are making the fire difficult to put out. The fire started on Monday near multiple campgrounds and summer homes.
paragraphs.cn: Seorang hakim federal telah mengeluarkan perintah nasional yang melarang kebijakan pemerintahan Trump untuk melakukan penangkapan di pengadilan imigrasi. Berdasarkan kebijakan pemerintahan Trump, pejabat federal mulai menahan para hadirin pengadilan tahun lalu. Sekarang, keputusan baru ini menyebut praktik tersebut sewenang-wenang. Kebakaran hutan di Utah telah menghanguskan sekitar 31.000 hektar lahan, dan api masih belum terkendali. Suhu tinggi dan angin kencang membuat pemadaman menjadi sulit. Kebakaran dimulai pada hari Senin di dekat beberapa perkemahan dan pondok.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And the NCAA has approved a sweeping overhaul of eligibility rules for student athletes. Division I athletes will now be allowed five years of competition in their college years, instead of four. Red shirts and waivers are now eliminated. And this new model goes into effect following this school year. And round one of the NBA draft is in the books. Any one of four top prospects could have been picked first by the Washington Wizards.
paragraphs.cn: NCAA telah menyetujui reformasi besar-besaran pada aturan kelayakan atlet mahasiswa. Atlet Divisi I sekarang akan diizinkan untuk berkompetisi selama lima tahun selama masa kuliah mereka, bukan empat tahun. Redshirt dan pengecualian sekarang dihapuskan. Model baru ini akan berlaku setelah tahun ajaran ini berakhir. Putaran pertama draft NBA telah selesai. Salah satu dari empat prospek teratas bisa dipilih oleh Washington Wizards dengan pilihan pertama.

paragraphs.speaker: Adam Silver
paragraphs.text: With the first pick in the 2026 NBA draft, the Washington Wizards select Anicet Dybantsa Jr. from Brigham Young University.
paragraphs.cn: Dalam draft NBA 2026, Washington Wizards memilih Anicet Dybantsa Jr. dari Brigham Young University dengan pilihan pertama.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: He is six foot nine and averages 25.5 points per game. He is the first player in more than 30 years to lead the NCAA in Division I scoring and get picked first in the NBA draft. Now, after the break on CNN THIS MORNING, progress in the war between Israel and Lebanon. Can Marco Rubio keep both sides engaged in peace talks? But first, President Trump on a collision course with Republican senators on Capitol Hill. They'll hash it out over lunch today.
paragraphs.cn: Dia memiliki tinggi 6 kaki 9 inci dan rata-rata mencetak 25,5 poin per game. Dia adalah pemain pertama dalam lebih dari 30 tahun yang memimpin daftar pencetak skor Divisi I NCAA dan terpilih sebagai pilihan pertama dalam draft NBA. Sekarang, setelah CNN This Morning, ada kemajuan dalam perang antara Israel dan Lebanon. Bisakah Marco Rubio membuat kedua belah pihak tetap terlibat dalam negosiasi damai? Tapi pertama-tama, Presiden Trump bentrok dengan senator Republik di Capitol Hill. Mereka akan menyelesaikan masalah ini dalam makan siang hari ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Who's going to leave with heartburn? And good morning from here in our nation's capital, as we take a live look at the sunrise over the dome.
paragraphs.cn: Siapa yang akan pergi dengan perasaan tidak puas? Selamat pagi, dari ibu kota negara kita, kami menyaksikan matahari terbit di atas kubah secara langsung.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: President Trump is failing to get his way in the Senate. He wants to pass his voting requirements bill, known as the SAVE Act, and he wants the Senate to end the filibuster to override Democrats to get it done. Senator Thom Tillis, a Republican, says that's not going to happen. SEN. THOM TILLIS (R-NC): This is a waste of time. It's a distraction, and it's not going to get -- it's not going to happen in this Congress. He's just got to understand some of the limits that we have here in the Senate. Talking's good.
paragraphs.cn: Presiden Trump tidak mendapatkan apa yang diinginkannya di Senat. Dia ingin meloloskan RUU persyaratan pemungutan suaranya, yaitu SAVE Act, dan ingin Senat mengakhiri filibuster untuk mengalahkan Demokrat. Senator Republik Tom Tillis mengatakan itu tidak akan terjadi. Senator Republik Tom Tillis (NC): Ini buang-buang waktu. Ini gangguan, dan tidak akan -- -- terjadi di Kongres ini. Dia harus memahami beberapa keterbatasan kami di Senat. Berbicara itu baik.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: SAVE Act's a waste of time.
paragraphs.cn: SAVE Act adalah buang-buang waktu.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Last night, several Republicans crossed party lines to vote for a war powers resolution, demanding that the president come to Congress if he wants to continue a war with Iran. Now, the battle over these bills is coming to a head for Senate leader John Thune. He's facing not just Trump, but rival Republicans gunning for his job and willing to use Trump's frustration to get it. So, what can we expect today? The group chat is back. I'm going to nerd out, because I used to cover the Senate. And Sen.
paragraphs.cn: Tadi malam, beberapa anggota Partai Republik memilih lintas partai untuk mendukung resolusi kekuasaan perang yang mengharuskan presiden untuk datang ke Kongres jika ingin melanjutkan perang dengan Iran. Sekarang, pertarungan seputar RUU ini menjadi momen kritis bagi Pemimpin Senat John Thune. Dia tidak hanya menghadapi tantangan dari Trump, tetapi juga saingan Partai Republik yang mengincar posisinya, yang bersedia memanfaatkan frustrasi Trump untuk tujuan mereka. Jadi, apa yang bisa kita harapkan hari ini? Group chat kembali. Saya akan mulai menjadi kutu buku karena saya dulu meliput Senat.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Thune was on X, hitting back at Mike Lee and others who were, like, Pass the SAVE Act and get rid of the filibuster. We're all frothy about this. And Thune was telling reporters, look, I have to deal with reality. And sometimes the alternative universe that is X does not reflect the facts on the ground. This is the old "Twitter is not real life" argument. And he says, "There are not the voters to nuke the filibuster. They aren't going to be ten Democratic votes to, all of a sudden, support the SAVE America Act.
paragraphs.cn: Thune membalas di X kepada Mike Lee dan lainnya yang mendukung SAVE Act dan penghapusan filibuster. Kami semua bersemangat tentang itu. Thune mengatakan kepada wartawan, dengar, saya harus realistis. Terkadang alam semesta paralel di X tidak mencerminkan kenyataan. Itu argumen lama 'Twitter bukan kehidupan nyata'. Dia berkata: 'Tidak ada cukup suara untuk menghapus filibuster. Tidak akan ada sepuluh senator Demokrat yang tiba-tiba mendukung SAVE Act.'

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Those are hard realities. And I think people at some point have to come to grips with that." People or Trump?
paragraphs.cn: Ini adalah kenyataan pahit. Saya pikir orang pada akhirnya harus menerimanya.' Orang-orang atau Trump?

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.text: Yes, I think this is really an interesting moment, especially for the filibuster. I would say that Trump is using this moment to say, look, it needs to be done away with to get what we want.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, saya pikir ini memang momen yang menarik, terutama untuk filibuster. Saya akan mengatakan Trump memanfaatkan momen ini untuk berkata, lihat, untuk mendapatkan apa yang kita inginkan, itu harus dihapuskan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But why use it with something they don't want and that they can't pass. Why not use it with something --
paragraphs.cn: Tapi mengapa menggunakannya untuk mendorong sesuatu yang tidak mereka inginkan dan tidak bisa lolos? Mengapa tidak menggunakannya untuk mendorong -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.text: Because I think that President Trump especially has actually had some criticism, even from his own voters, that he hasn't paid enough attention to domestic politics, that he's been focused on international affairs. The SAVE America Act, which is very popular amongst the Republican base, is something that needs to happen. And so, Trump, I think, is getting pressure from his own voters. And he is now pressuring the Senate, thinking, Well --
paragraphs.cn: Karena saya pikir Presiden Trump khususnya mendapat kritik, bahkan dari pemilihnya sendiri, bahwa dia tidak cukup fokus pada politik dalam negeri dan lebih fokus pada urusan internasional. SAVE Act sangat populer di kalangan pemilih basis Partai Republik, ini adalah sesuatu yang harus dilakukan. Jadi, saya pikir Trump sedang mendapat tekanan dari pemilihnya sendiri. Dia sekarang menekan Senat, berpikir, baiklah -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But why not press an economic thing?
paragraphs.cn: Tapi mengapa tidak memberikan tekanan ekonomi?

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.text: -- what have we been doing these last six months -- six months?
paragraphs.cn: -- -- apa yang kita lakukan selama enam bulan terakhir? Enam bulan?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Like, if you're -- like, if their problem is focusing on domestic policy, why not do something that actually affects their pockets?
paragraphs.cn: Misalnya, jika masalah mereka adalah fokus pada kebijakan dalam negeri, mengapa tidak melakukan sesuatu yang benar-benar mempengaruhi dompet mereka?

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.text: I think because Republican voters also see elections as mattering. They -- they very much support voter I.D. as a --
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir karena pemilih Partai Republik juga menganggap pemilu itu penting. Mereka sangat mendukung verifikasi identitas pemilih sebagai -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, he's putting them on that. What do you see?
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, dia mengarahkan mereka untuk fokus pada itu. Bagaimana menurutmu?

paragraphs.speaker: Brownstein
paragraphs.text: I see meet the new boss, same as the old boss. What was the issue on which there was the most pressure to end the filibuster during Joe Biden's presidency? HR-1, voting reform. The Democratic vision of what national voting rules should be. And ultimately, they could not -- they did not have enough votes to pass to end the filibuster in the Senate. Manchin and Sinema and others who were mostly unnamed. To -- to this point, there is not a majority in the Senate on either side to end the filibuster.
paragraphs.cn: Yang saya lihat adalah bos baru sama dengan bos lama. Selama masa kepresidenan Joe Biden, masalah mana yang paling menekan untuk mengakhiri filibuster? HR-1, reformasi pemilu. Visi Demokrat tentang bagaimana aturan pemilu nasional seharusnya. Pada akhirnya, mereka tidak bisa -- -- mereka tidak memiliki cukup suara di Senat untuk mengakhiri filibuster. Manchin, Sinema, dan lainnya yang sebagian besar tidak disebutkan namanya. Sejauh ini, tidak ada mayoritas di kedua partai di Senat yang mendukung penghapusan filibuster.

paragraphs.speaker: Brownstein
paragraphs.text: And as long as there isn't, you can't impose a national voting regime on red states and blue states.
paragraphs.cn: Selama tidak ada mayoritas yang mendukung, Anda tidak bisa memaksakan sistem pemilu nasional pada negara bagian merah dan biru.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, one other thing. Rick Scott of Florida, I was reading the reporting, was the one who might have invited Trump. You're nodding. You heard this, too. Invited Trump to this lunch. Thune was like, Oh, yes, I, too, want him at this lunch. And so now they're going to have to face Trump. You've been in leader meetings, I know, on House side, but how does this go down?
paragraphs.cn: Dan satu hal lagi. Rick Scott dari Florida, saya membaca laporan, mungkin dialah yang mengundang Trump. Anda mengangguk. Anda juga mendengarnya. Mengundang Trump untuk makan siang ini. Thune berkata, oh ya, saya juga ingin dia hadir dalam makan siang ini. Jadi sekarang mereka harus menghadapi Trump. Anda pernah menghadiri pertemuan pimpinan, saya tahu, di sisi DPR, tapi bagaimana ini akan berjalan?

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: Oh, it will be -- whatever happens in the meeting will be leaked to the press before the meeting even ends. That's No. 1.
paragraphs.cn: Oh, apa pun yang terjadi dalam pertemuan akan bocor ke media sebelum pertemuan berakhir. Itu poin pertama.

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Seawright
paragraphs.text: No. 2. But I think this whole idea of the SAVE America Act, or Voter Suppression Act.
paragraphs.cn: Poin kedua. Tapi saya pikir seluruh gagasan SAVE Act atau Voter Suppression Act -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And I'm going to put up while you're speaking, what's in it so people can see.
paragraphs.cn: Sementara Anda berbicara, saya akan menampilkan kontennya agar semua orang bisa melihat.

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: This Voter Suppression Act is a larger narrative that the president and his party are trying to implement, to strike fear and intimidation and voter suppression into the midterms. You add this with redistricting. You add this with his executive order on banning mail-in voting. There's a number of things they're trying to do to shape what the midterms look like. I've often called it the "if you can't beat them, cheat them" strategy.
paragraphs.cn: Undang-Undang Penekanan Pemilih ini adalah bagian dari narasi yang lebih besar yang coba diterapkan oleh presiden dan partainya untuk menciptakan ketakutan, intimidasi, dan penekanan pemilih dalam pemilu paruh waktu. Ditambah dengan redistricting dan perintah eksekutifnya tentang larangan pemungutan suara melalui pos. Mereka mencoba membentuk wajah pemilu paruh waktu melalui serangkaian cara. Saya sering menyebutnya strategi 'kalau tidak bisa menang, curangi saja'.

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: And most reasonable Republicans know that this is not the way to go if they want to be successful in the midterms.
paragraphs.cn: Kebanyakan Republikan yang rasional tahu bahwa ini bukan cara yang benar jika mereka ingin menang dalam pemilu paruh waktu.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But can I underscore Ron's point, which is that under Democrats, people didn't want to mess with it. And under Republicans now, people don't want to mess with it. They see voting as the province of the states. They already have watched this redistricting war. And this brings me to this point. Why is it with a war powers vote -- I'm going to show you the Republicans who crossed the aisle with voting. Why are these the trigger points that have inspired a certain courage in the Senate against Trump?
paragraphs.cn: Tapi bisakah saya menekankan poin Ron: ketika Demokrat berkuasa, orang tidak ingin campur tangan dalam pemilu; sekarang Republikan berkuasa, orang juga tidak ingin campur tangan. Mereka menganggap pemungutan suara adalah urusan negara bagian. Mereka sudah menyaksikan perjuangan redistricting. Ini membawa saya ke pertanyaan: mengapa dalam pemungutan suara kekuasaan perang -- saya akan menunjukkan Republikan yang memilih lintas partai -- ini menjadi pemicu yang mendorong seseorang di Senat untuk berani melawan Trump?

paragraphs.speaker: Brownstein
paragraphs.text: Sixty-nine percent of Americans in a poll on Sunday said the war was not worth the costs. I mean, that's -- you know, you can find a lot of courage in a -- in a 69 percent issue. And it is still like, even on the voting, the vast majority of each party's legislators are willing to vote for setting national rules that would -- that would affect all 50 states, but not enough. I mean, it is -- you know, I don't know how many Republicans would vote to end the filibuster to pass the SAVE Act.
paragraphs.cn: Jajak pendapat hari Minggu menunjukkan 69% orang Amerika menganggap perang ini tidak sepadan dengan biayanya. Maksud saya, Anda tahu, dalam isu dengan dukungan 69%, mudah untuk menemukan keberanian. Dan bahkan dalam isu pemungutan suara, mayoritas besar legislator dari kedua partai bersedia memilih untuk menetapkan aturan nasional yang mempengaruhi semua 50 negara bagian, tapi itu belum cukup. Maksud saya, saya tidak tahu berapa banyak Republikan yang akan memilih untuk mengakhiri filibuster guna meloloskan SAVE Act.

paragraphs.speaker: Brownstein
paragraphs.text: But it's -- you know, it's a lot. It's probably like 45 or 47.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi Anda tahu, jumlahnya tidak sedikit. Mungkin 45 atau 47 orang.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes, we're being pretty vague. There are things in here --
paragraphs.cn: Ya, kita bicara agak samar. Ada beberapa hal di sini --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- Democrats can campaign on. As a woman with a hyphenated name, I've been watching this closely. I don't want to get to the polls and be told, well, wait a second. Are you who you say you are? It feels like this is an issue that can be easily brought to voters, a la Antjuan, but that Republicans -- I mean, if Thune is saying it's not there --
paragraphs.cn: -- yang bisa digunakan Demokrat untuk berkampanye. Sebagai seorang wanita dengan nama yang menggunakan tanda hubung, saya terus memantau ini. Saya tidak mau sampai di tempat pemungutan suara dan diberitahu: tunggu, apakah Anda benar-benar orang yang Anda katakan? Rasanya masalah ini mudah dipahami pemilih seperti yang dikatakan Antwan, tapi Republikan -- maksud saya, jika Thune bilang ini tidak ada --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- are the people going after him, just trying to curry favor with Trump, to show, Look, I would have done this on your agenda if it was me.
paragraphs.cn: -- mereka yang menyerangnya hanya ingin menyenangkan Trump, menunjukkan: lihat, jika saya yang jadi, saya akan mengikuti agenda Anda.

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.text: But some of the issues, like, for instance, voter I.D., is extremely --
paragraphs.cn: Tapi beberapa masalah, seperti ID pemilih, sangat --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: No, no, answer my question.
paragraphs.cn: Tidak, tidak, jawab pertanyaan saya.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Is it a power move on Thune to basically bring him before the president and tell everyone, look what he's not doing? I would have done it.
paragraphs.cn: Apakah ini tekanan kekuasaan terhadap Thune, pada dasarnya membawanya ke hadapan presiden, memberitahu semua orang, lihat apa yang tidak dia lakukan? Saya akan melakukannya.

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.text: No question. And this is sort of the independence that the Senate has sort of always had as -- as a body; sees itself as outside the president. I think you get a little less of that opposition in the House.
paragraphs.cn: Tidak diragukan lagi. Ini agak mirip dengan independensi Senat sebagai lembaga independen yang menganggap dirinya terpisah dari presiden. Saya pikir di DPR, oposisi semacam ini akan lebih sedikit.

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: Yes. I don't -- I think you have convenient independence. Because more than not, they have aligned themselves --
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Saya tidak berpikir -- saya pikir ini adalah independensi yang nyaman. Karena sebagian besar waktu, mereka sejalan dengan --

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: -- with Donald Trump on almost every single issue. And that's why Senate Republicans are so unpopular, because the president is underwater on every single issue. I also think Thune understands now that the affordability crisis is right at their doorstep, and they're taking responsibility for it. And the Donald Trump agenda is very unpopular --
paragraphs.cn: -- Donald Trump di hampir semua masalah. Itulah mengapa Senator Republikan sangat tidak populer, karena presiden kalah dalam setiap masalah. Saya juga berpikir Thune sekarang mengerti bahwa krisis keterjangkauan sudah di depan mata, dan mereka yang bertanggung jawab. Dan agenda Donald Trump sangat tidak populer --

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: -- amongst the American people, including the savings (ph).
paragraphs.cn: -- di kalangan rakyat Amerika, termasuk dalam hal tabungan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: They also passed a housing bill last night, bipartisan. We're not going to hear about it, because they're going to be -- So, it's like that kind of thing where it's like, wait, you have an item you could be promoting to the voter. Look, we got a bipartisan housing bill. Instead, you're like, really, I want to go after all these different voting things. How about we spend time on that?
paragraphs.cn: Mereka juga meloloskan RUU perumahan bipartisan tadi malam. Kita tidak akan mendengar ini, karena mereka akan -- jadi, seperti situasi itu: tunggu, kalian punya isu yang bisa dijual ke pemilih. Lihat, kami meloloskan RUU perumahan bipartisan. Sebaliknya, kalian malah mau, sungguh, saya akan mengurus semua urusan pemungutan suara yang berbeda ini. Kenapa kita tidak menghabiskan waktu untuk itu?

paragraphs.speaker: Brownstein
paragraphs.text: The thing that's frustrating for senators in both parties is they are much less able than when I first got to Washington in the '80s to swim against the current. You know.
paragraphs.cn: Yang membuat frustrasi Senator bipartisan adalah, dibandingkan ketika saya pertama kali datang ke Washington di tahun 80-an, kemampuan mereka untuk melawan arus sekarang jauh berkurang. Anda tahu.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. There's no reward for that.
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Tidak ada imbalannya.

paragraphs.speaker: Brownstein
paragraphs.text: If you look -- if you look, there are 25 states that voted three times for Donald Trump. Right now, there are 50 Republican senators in those 25. They have every Senate seats. Nineteen states voted three times against him. Democrats have every one of those Senate seats except for Susan Collins. It is very hard for a Senate candidate, no matter how strong or popular they are personally, to win on ground that usually votes the other way for president. So, in some ways, they are dependent variables.
paragraphs.cn: Jika Anda lihat -- jika Anda lihat, ada 25 negara bagian yang tiga kali memilih Donald Trump. Sekarang, 25 negara bagian ini memiliki 50 Senator Republikan. Mereka menduduki semua kursi Senat. 19 negara bagian tiga kali memilih menentangnya. Kecuali Susan Collins, Demokrat menduduki semua kursi Senat itu. Bagi kandidat Senator, tidak peduli seberapa kuat atau populer mereka secara pribadi, sangat sulit untuk menang di daerah pemilihan yang biasanya memilih presiden dari partai lain. Jadi, sampai batas tertentu, mereka tergantung pada variabel.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: They are, but there's also --
paragraphs.cn: Mereka memang, tapi ada juga --

paragraphs.speaker: Brownstein
paragraphs.text: In the future no matter how much they kind of, you know --
paragraphs.cn: Ke depannya, apa pun yang mereka lakukan, Anda tahu --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: There's also now a small --
paragraphs.cn: Sekarang juga ada sedikit --

paragraphs.speaker: Brownstein
paragraphs.text: -- plume (ph) and peacock.
paragraphs.cn: -- bulu-bulu dan merak.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- caucus of people burned by Trump in the Republican caucus.
paragraphs.cn: -- sekelompok orang di kaukus Republikan yang terbakar oleh Trump.

paragraphs.speaker: Brownstein
paragraphs.text: Yes. They're on their way out.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。他们正在离开。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And they're on their way out.
paragraphs.cn: 他们正在离开。

paragraphs.speaker: Brownstein
paragraphs.text: They're on their way out.
paragraphs.cn: 他们正在离开。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But on their way out, they may say, I'll cast a few votes the way I want.
paragraphs.cn: 但在离开的路上，他们可能会说，我会按自己的意愿投几票。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: That's how -- that's how courage is built. OK, you guys, thank you for this. Straight ahead on CNN THIS MORNING, NATO sending its Trump whisperer. So, can the secretary-general keep the alliance together? Plus, how much has the A.I. industry spent in just one congressional race? Why one election has -- was worth tens of millions in influence. var CNN = CNN || {};
